title: Percolation of sign clusters for the Gaussian free field I
speaker: Pierre-Francois Rodriguez
abstract: We consider level sets of the Gaussian free field on the $d$-dimensional lattice, for $d>2$, above a given real-valued height $h$. This defines a percolation model with strong, algebraically decaying correlations. We prove a conjecture of Lebowitz asserting that the sign clusters of this field, i.e. the level sets above height $h=0$, contain a unique infinite connected component.

proof_stages.label: GFF level-set percolation problem and target theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines the lattice Gaussian free field in dimension at least three, formulates percolation of the level set above height h via the critical parameter h*, reviews known nontriviality results, and states the conjectural goal h* > 0.

proof_stages.label: three-step proof strategy
proof_stages.summary: The proof is organized as: explicitly construct an augmented infinite cluster above a small negative level, prove this cluster is robust, and perturb it to obtain percolation above a small positive level.

proof_stages.label: random interlacement skeleton
proof_stages.summary: Random interlacements are constructed from a Poisson cloud of random-walk trajectories using equilibrium measure and capacity, yielding monotone connected infinite sets with local times that will serve as the explicit skeleton.

proof_stages.label: cable-system lift and Lupu isomorphism
proof_stages.summary: The lattice is replaced by the cable system so that the GFF, interlacements, and local times become continuous, and Lupu's almost-sure isomorphism couples local times and squares of shifted Gaussian fields while resolving the sign information through continuous sign clusters.

proof_stages.label: explicit infinite cluster above a negative level
proof_stages.summary: Because the interlacement is connected and has positive local time, Lupu's identity forces the shifted field to have a constant sign on it, giving an explicit infinite continuous cluster above level -sqrt(2u).

proof_stages.label: robust controlled subset of the interlacement
proof_stages.summary: A stability theorem, whose proof is deferred, asserts that for any coupling of the continuous GFF and interlacements one can find an open infinite connected subset A of the interlacement on whose one-neighborhood the field is bounded by a slowly growing K(u).

proof_stages.label: midpoint factorization and local upward-coupling lemma
proof_stages.summary: Cutting cables at their midpoints factorizes the cable system into crosses; conditioning on midpoint values, a Brownian-bridge barrier estimate and Bernoulli domination couple a new discrete GFF so that a controlled negative-level crossing forces the lattice vertex above +sqrt(2u).

proof_stages.label: global perturbation to a positive-level cluster
proof_stages.summary: Applying the robust-set theorem to the Lupu coupling, every lattice vertex of the constructed continuous cluster satisfies the local lemma along some controlled half-edge, so its trace on Z^d lies in an infinite cluster of a discrete GFF above a positive level.
